question: A new recommendation ranking model increased revenue by 5% and CTR by 7%, but 7-day retention dropped by 2%. Should we fully launch?
The treatment group has 30% more users than control in a planned 50/50 experiment. Can we trust the result?
A feed experiment increased CTR, but conversion rate decreased and revenue per user is flat. Should we launch based on clicks?
A recommendation experiment shows revenue increased, conversion improved, retention stable, complaints stable, and no guardrail regression. What is the launch decision?
Overall revenue improved, but high-value users had lower retention and more complaints. Should this ship to everyone?
A feature was launched only in one city with no randomized control, and we have pre/post market data. Is this a normal A/B test?
Revenue increased, but complaint rate and report rate increased too. What should the launch memo recommend?
The ranking change was enabled in one market because operations chose it, not because users were randomized. How should we evaluate impact?
An experiment has sample ratio mismatch at p < 0.01, but treatment revenue looks 8% higher. Should we launch?
The primary metric improved, but the confidence interval includes meaningful downside on retention. What should we do next?
An ads experiment increased CTR but lowered advertiser conversion quality and ROAS. Is CTR enough to justify launch?
A marketplace ranking model increased buyer revenue but reduced exposure for small sellers and may hurt supply health. What decision is appropriate?
A model improves watch time during the first day, but users are less likely to return after a week. How should a recommendation team interpret this?
Control and treatment are balanced, revenue is significantly up, retention is stable, complaints are stable, and segment checks show no harm. Should we launch?
The treatment effect is positive for new users but negative for returning high-value users. What rollout strategy should we consider?
Exposure logs are missing for one app version and treatment has fewer eligible users than expected. Can we interpret the lift?
A commerce recommendation test increased add-to-cart clicks but lowered purchase conversion. What metrics should be checked before launch?
A policy changed in one country during a holiday season, and another country might be a comparison group. What analysis design fits?
The experiment improved revenue, but the team did not predefine a primary metric or guardrails. What should happen before launch?
Ad load increased revenue, but user complaints and unsubscribe rate worsened. What is the safest recommendation?
A ranking test appears positive overall, but Android users in Germany have a large negative retention effect. What should we inspect?
An experiment is a clean win: revenue is significantly up, conversion is up, all guardrails stable, and SRM passed. What should the decision be?
Treatment assignment changed halfway through the experiment and the exposure population drifted. Should we use the measured revenue lift?
A recommendation model increased revenue by making item exposure less diverse and concentrating traffic on a few sellers. What risks should the memo flag?
Assignment counts look balanced, but treatment impression logs are missing for Android app version 9.2. Can we trust conversion lift from that segment?
The team changed conversion event schema on day 6 only for treatment traffic. Should we use post-change conversion improvement as launch evidence?
A test shows strong positive lift, but analysis excludes users who did not trigger the new module and treatment likely changes trigger probability. Is this causal evidence?
Treatment adoption was stopped early after repeated daily peeking when p-value crossed 0.05, without sequential correction. Can we treat this as a clean win?
A recommender improves CTR and watch time, but hide/report and skip-after-click both rise. Should the team launch broadly?
Offline NDCG improved, but online successful sessions and retention are flat. What should we conclude?
A recommendation policy boosts purchases, but refund-adjusted margin declines and repeat purchase worsens. Should this be treated as a win?
A feed ranking change helps users in dense markets, but sparse markets show lower availability and worse return rate. What rollout strategy fits best?
Overall retention is flat, but a pre-specified strategic segment of high-LTV users drops by 0.8pp beyond tolerance. Should we launch globally?
Only one exploratory segment out of 30 scanned looks positive, and no interaction test is provided. Can we launch only for that segment?
A feature benefits new users but harms existing users, and targeting is technically feasible. What is the safest recommendation now?
The claimed winning segment is defined as users who clicked the new module, and click behavior is affected by treatment. Is this valid segmentation evidence for launch?
Aggregate metrics are positive, but Android users in one major market show a large reliability regression and crash-rate spike. What should the memo recommend?
A pricing policy changed only in one country while comparable countries remained unchanged. What method should be used before making causal claims?
Rollout timing is staggered across regions, but analysis uses naive TWFE without cohort diagnostics. Is the causal conclusion reliable enough to launch?
A policy appears effective, but treated and control groups already had diverging pre-trends before launch. Should we trust the DiD estimate?
A marketplace intervention likely causes spillovers from treated sellers to untreated sellers. Is standard user-level A/B enough?
One city receives treatment and no comparable control city exists, but long pre-period data is available. What design should be considered instead of simple DiD?
Ad ranking raises platform revenue by 6%, but advertiser ROAS falls by 4% and small advertiser impression share declines. Should we ship broadly?
Retargeting campaign shows 10x attributed ROAS, but there is no randomized holdout or Ghost Ads evidence. Should we scale now?
Ad load increase improves short-term RPM, but hide/report rises above threshold and high-value user retention declines. What launch decision is most responsible?
An ad-format test improves CTR by 15%, but post-click conversion quality is down and refund-adjusted value drops. Is this a clean win?
Auction reserve-price changes were tested with user-level randomization, and treatment likely shifts control auction prices. What should we do before trusting impact?
Spend and conversions rose in treatment, but spend ramped 12% faster because pacing settings differed by variant. Can we interpret lift as true ad quality gain?
We only know CTR improved by 9%. No retention, no conversion, no revenue, and no guardrail metrics were collected. Should we fully launch now?
A memo claims retention down less than 3% is always acceptable for launch, but provides no policy threshold source. Is that claim supported?
The report says treatment had both 50/50 assignment and severe SRM at p<0.001 for the same frozen dataset. Can we trust this result without reconciliation?
The team only reports treatment means and no control baseline, uncertainty, sample size, or experiment duration. Can we decide launch confidently?
A marketplace ranking change claims buyer gains, but seller-side exposure, earnings, and retention are entirely unavailable. Should this be a broad launch recommendation?
expected_concepts: avoid broad launch with missing key side

question: An ads team reports 12x attributed ROAS but provides no holdout, no Ghost Ads, and no incrementality estimate. Should we treat this as causal proof to scale?
A policy was implemented in one country during a major holiday and a pricing migration started the same week, with no clean control or pre-trend diagnostics. Is a causal claim reliable now?
The only strong effect appears in a post-treatment segment defined as users who clicked the new card, and 40 exploratory segments were scanned. Can we launch for that segment directly?
